title: 10 best herbal creams for overactive bladder

remedy_properties: Urothelial protection: Bearberry contains compounds like arbutin, which helps to protect the urothelial lining of the bladder and urethra from damage, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Antispasmodic activity: The anthraquinones present in bearberry have been shown to relax smooth muscle tissue, including the detrusor muscle in the bladder, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate symptoms of urge incontinence.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Bearberry's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms and improve overall bladder function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arctostaphylos uva ursi, also known as bearberry, creams helps with overactive bladder because it contains arbutin, a natural compound that has been traditionally used to treat urinary issues. The arbutin in bearberry creams works by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the bladder, which helps to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as urgency, frequency, and incontinence. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of arbutin may also help to relax the bladder muscles and improve bladder control. This makes bearberry creams a natural and effective solution for managing overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: Acts as a natural antispasmodic to relax the bladder muscles and reduce the frequency of urination.
Arctiin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder, thus alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
Arbutin: Has a mild astringent effect, which helps to tighten the bladder muscles and reduce the urgency to urinate.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their astringent properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
Barks: Utilized for their tannin content, which can help contract the muscles of the bladder and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Fruits (Berries): Used due to their antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the bladder muscles, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of uva ursi leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency in mid-summer.
Dry the freshly harvested uva ursi in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for eight hours.
Steep 50 grams of dried uva ursi in 250 milliliters of coconut oil at 60 degrees celsius for two hours.
Strain the uva ursi infused coconut oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids in an hour.
Blend the strained coconut oil with 250 milliliters of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter to create a smooth cream in five minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Saw palmetto's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like frequent urination.

Sarperpine diacetate inhibition: Saw palmetto extracts contain a compound called serenoilic acid that inhibits 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme responsible for converting testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which has been linked to overactive bladder symptoms in men and women.

Muscle relaxant properties: The beta-sitosterol content in saw palmetto cream may help relax the muscles of the urinary tract, reducing muscle spasms and contractions that can lead to frequent urination.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens, also known as saw palmetto, creams helps with overactive bladder because it may help relax the bladder muscles and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in Serenoa repens have been shown to have a mild antispasmodic effect, which can help to reduce the frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Serenoa repens may help to soothe the bladder and reduce irritation, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms. This can lead to improved bladder control and a reduction in symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscle of the bladder, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination associated with overactive bladder.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids, particularly ferulic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help protect the bladder from damage and reduce the symptoms of overactive bladder.

Triterpenoid Saponins: Triterpenoid saponins, such as serenoa saponins, may help to relax the smooth muscle of the bladder and reduce the contractions that cause urgency and frequency in overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part used due to their high concentration of saw palmetto extract, which is believed to help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Roots: Roots are also used, as they contain bioactive compounds that may help regulate bladder function and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Seeds: Seeds are another part used, as they contain a high amount of fatty acids and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried serenoa repens roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any impurities.
Macerate the serenoa repens roots in 100ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil at a 1:3 ratio for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 10ml of the herbal extract with 90ml of a base cream containing 20% w/w glycerin and 10% w/w beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes to achieve a smooth and even consistency.

remedy_properties: Muco-adhesive properties: Marshmallow root has natural mucilage that forms a protective film on the urinary tract lining, soothing and calming irritated tissues to reduce frequency and urgency of urination.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the bladder and urethra, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like pain and discomfort during urination.

Antispasmodic properties: Marshmallow root has been traditionally used to relax smooth muscle tissues, including those in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate spasms and cramps that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with overactive bladder because of its soothing and protective properties. The cream's active compounds, such as mucilages, work to calm the bladder and urethral tissues, reducing inflammation and irritation. This helps to regulate bladder contractions and frequency, providing relief from symptoms of overactive bladder. The cream's ability to create a protective barrier also helps to prevent further irritation and inflammation, promoting a healthier bladder environment.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and calm the bladder and urinary tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the bladder and urinary tract, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, particularly ursolic acid, have been shown to have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle, which can help to calm the bladder and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder, such as urgency and frequency.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are used due to their high content of mucilages, which provide soothing and protective properties for the urinary system.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation and provide comfort to the bladder and urinary tract.
Stems: The stems are used for their similar mucilaginous properties to the roots, providing soothing and protective effects on the urinary system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried althaea officinalis root in early spring when the plant is in full bloom.
Infuse the dried root in 500 milliliters of hot water for 10 to 15 minutes to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction and add 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil to it.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until the beeswax has melted completely.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it into a clean container for storage.

remedy_properties: Astringent and anti-inflammatory properties: Goldenrod's astringent compounds help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequency and urgency.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: The antioxidants and antimicrobial agents present in goldenrod creams may help protect against bacterial adhesion to the bladder wall, reducing the risk of UTIs and other infections that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.

Muscle-relaxing and anti-spasmodic properties: Goldenrod's ability to relax smooth muscle tissues may help reduce spasms and contractions in the urinary tract, providing relief from frequent urination and urgency.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Solidago virgaurea, also known as goldenrod, creams helps with overactive bladder because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The herb has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the muscles, reducing spasms and inflammation in the urinary tract. The flavonoids present in Solidago virgaurea may help relax the bladder muscles, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, its diuretic properties may help improve bladder function and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, providing relief and improving overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Solidago virgaurea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, potentially alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Solidago virgaurea may help relax the bladder muscles and improve bladder function by reducing the contraction of the detrusor muscle, a common issue in overactive bladder.

Terpenes: Terpenes in Solidago virgaurea may have a relaxing effect on the bladder and urinary tract, helping to reduce muscle spasms and improve bladder control, thus alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to help soothe the urinary tract.
Flowers: Used for their diuretic properties to help increase urine production and relieve urinary issues.
Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial properties to help prevent infections and promote healing in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried solidago virgaurea flowers and 100 grams of base oil such as sweet almond oil.
Infuse the dried flowers in the base oil in a double boiler at 50°c for 2 hours to create a herbal extract.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids to obtain a clear herbal oil.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter to the herbal oil and melt at 60°c for 10 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into a container and let it solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes to form a cream.

remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Herbal corn creams containing ingredients like Aloe vera, Chamomile, or Marshmallow root can help soothe and calm irritated skin around the vulva, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms.

Antibacterial and Antifungal Activity: Ingredients like Tea tree oil, Echinacea, or Goldenseal in herbal corn creams can prevent infections that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms by combating bacteria and fungi that may be contributing to the condition.

Calming and Relaxing Properties: Herbal ingredients like Valerian root, Passionflower, or Lavender in corn creams can promote relaxation and calmness, helping to reduce stress and anxiety which are known triggers for overactive bladder episodes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zea mays, also known as corn, creams helps with overactive bladder because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that soothe and relax the bladder muscles. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zea mays cream reduce bladder irritation, while its calming effects help to regulate bladder contractions. Additionally, the cream's ability to increase blood flow to the bladder area may also contribute to its therapeutic effects, promoting a healthier and more balanced bladder function. This natural approach may provide relief from symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: Avenanthramides are a group of polyphenolic compounds found in Zea mays. They may help alleviate OAB symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, which can contribute to OAB.
Caffeic acid: Caffeic acid, a phenolic compound present in Zea mays, may help relax the bladder muscles and reduce bladder contractions, which can lead to OAB symptoms.
Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid, another phenolic compound found in Zea mays, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the bladder from damage and reduce OAB symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make creams for overactive bladder due to their high concentration of lignans, which have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the bladder muscles.
Seeds: Used to make creams for overactive bladder due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the bladder.
Seeds: Used to make creams for overactive bladder due to their content of diosgenin, a natural estrogen-like compound that can help regulate bladder function.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried zea mays powder and 100 grams of beeswax for the herbal zea mays cream.
Combine 10 grams of dried zea mays powder with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 100 grams of beeswax to the mixture in the double boiler and heat until melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°f.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound found in licorice root, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to symptoms of overactive bladder.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice cream contains flavonoids like isoliquiritigenin, which have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the bladder wall, helping to reduce spasms and improve urine storage capacity.
Saponins: Licorice root saponins have been found to have a mild antispasmodic effect, which can help alleviate sudden, frequent urges to urinate by reducing muscle contractions in the urinary tract.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with overactive bladder because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The root extract in these creams has been shown to calm the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and frequency of urination. Additionally, licorice root is rich in antioxidants, which help to protect the bladder from oxidative stress and promote overall bladder health. By applying these creams topically, individuals can experience relief from overactive bladder symptoms, improving their quality of life and reducing the need for medication.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with overactive bladder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, thereby improving its function and reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
Licagenin: It acts as an antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to relax the bladder muscles and reduce spasms associated with overactive bladder.

Isoliquiritigenin: It has a protective effect on the bladder by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and may also have a direct antispasmodic effect on the bladder muscles, helping to alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate bladder symptoms.
Barks: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing bladder irritation and reducing overactive bladder symptoms.
Leaves: Employed for their calming effects, which help in relaxing the bladder muscles and reducing the urge to urinate frequently.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour, or until the roots are fully infused.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl to remove the roots.
Mix 100 grams of emulsifying wax, 100 grams of candelilla wax, and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Combine the infused glycyrrhiza glabra liquid with the melted wax mixture and blend until well combined.

remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail cream contains high levels of silica, which helps to strengthen and tone the muscles of the bladder wall, reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in horsetail cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the bladder and surrounding tissues that may contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense, also known as field horsetail, creams helps with overactive bladder because it contains high levels of silica and flavonoids, which have been shown to strengthen and tone the muscles of the pelvic floor. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Equisetum arvense may help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the bladder and urethra, further alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Furanosesquiterpenes: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce bladder spasms and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic and caffeic acid, have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect the bladder from damage and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, have been reported to possess antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relax the bladder muscles and reduce bladder spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make creams due to their rich content of flavonoids, which have a soothing and calming effect on the bladder muscles.
Rhyzomes: They are used to make creams due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce bladder irritation.
Stems: They are used to make creams due to their content of lignans, which have a relaxant effect on the bladder muscles and help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of fresh equisetum arvense shoots on a dry day at dawn for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested shoots in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 8 hours to preserve the herbs.
Steep 10 grams of dried equisetum arvense in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean container.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with the infused oil in a double boiler and heat until fully melted and combined.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxadiol, help reduce inflammation in the bladder and urethra, which can contribute to symptoms of overactive bladder.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, like vitamin C and beta-carotene, protect the bladder lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.

Soothing and calming properties: Dandelion's soothing and calming effects, attributed to compounds like flavonoids and triterpenoids, help relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing frequency and urgency of urination associated with overactive bladder.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with overactive bladder because they contain diuretic properties that aid in urine production and flow. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce irritation and swelling in the bladder and surrounding tissues, promoting a smoother functioning of the urinary system. Additionally, the herb's natural muscle relaxant properties calm the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and contractions that contribute to overactive bladder symptoms, providing relief and comfort to those affected.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce bladder inflammation and irritation, thereby alleviating overactive bladder symptoms.
Taraxeryl acetate: This triterpenoid ester has been shown to have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle, which may help calm bladder spasms and reduce the urge to urinate frequently.

Phenolic acids (e.g., caffeic acid, ferulic acid): These antioxidants may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the bladder, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms, such as urinary frequency and urgency.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale contain inulin and other compounds that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.

Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient amount of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves about 50 grams.
Clean and dry the plant material thoroughly using a clean towel or cheesecloth within 10 minutes.
Combine the dried plant material with 100 grams of vegetable oil in a heat-proof bowl and heat it gently for 30 minutes at 50 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain a clear liquid extract and discard the solids immediately.
Mix the extract with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of emulsifier to create a smooth and even cream within 15 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the bladder, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as frequent urination and urgency.
Antioxidant: Burdock cream's antioxidant properties help protect the bladder lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.

Anti-spasmodic: Burdock cream's anti-spasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the bladder and urethra, reducing spasms that can contribute to frequent urination, urgency, and other overactive bladder symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arctium lappa, also known as burdock, creams helps with overactive bladder because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the bladder muscles. The cream's active compounds, such as inulin and arctiin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the bladder, allowing for more controlled urination. Additionally, Arctium lappa's natural astringent properties help to reduce muscle spasms, providing relief from the sudden and frequent urges to urinate associated with overactive bladder. This natural remedy offers a potential alternative to pharmaceutical treatments.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help relax the bladder muscles and reduce the frequency of urination, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.
Phenolic acids: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the bladder and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

Alkaloids (particularly Berberine): Berberine, an alkaloid present in Arctium lappa, has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles of the bladder, reducing muscle spasms and frequency of urination.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, which has been shown to have potential benefits for bladder health and urinary incontinence.
Seeds: Contain mucilages, which can help soothe and calm the bladder, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
Leaves: Known to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder and improve bladder health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of fresh arctium lappa roots and dry them in a warm place for 2-3 days.
Grind 1 cup of dried arctium lappa roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 1 cup of arctium lappa powder with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain 1 cup of herbal infused oil.
Mix 1 cup of herbal infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter to create 1 pound of cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream, including vitamins A and C, help protect the urinary tract cells from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the risk of bladder spasms and urge incontinence.

Soothing and calming effects: Pot marigold's natural soothing and calming properties can help reduce bladder irritability and alleviate symptoms such as frequency, urgency, and nocturia associated with overactive bladder.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with overactive bladder because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urinary tract. This can lead to improved bladder control and a decrease in symptoms of overactive bladder, such as frequent urination and urgency. The cream's ability to promote healing and reduce discomfort may also contribute to its potential benefits for individuals with overactive bladder.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These pigments help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the bladder, which may contribute to the symptoms of overactive bladder.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help soothe and calm the bladder, reducing spasms and overactive contractions.

Flavonoids (specifically, Quercetin): This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce bladder inflammation and oxidative stress, leading to a decrease in overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract.

Leaves: They are also used to make creams for overactive bladder, as they contain sesquiterpenes and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help relax the muscles in the urinary tract.

Stems: Although less commonly used, stems of Calendula officinalis are also used in some creams for overactive bladder, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse calendula flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:3 for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Melt 30 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the infused oil and the melted wax mixture in a bowl and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow to cool and set for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper creams helps with overactive bladder because it contains a unique blend of herbs that relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency. The cream's active ingredients, such as chamomile and uva ursi, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the bladder lining, calming irritation and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's ability to increase blood flow to the area helps to strengthen the muscles around the urethra, providing a sense of control and relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Saw Palmetto: May help relax the urinary tract muscles to reduce frequency and urgency of urination.
Marshmallow Root: May soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms.

remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots contain a rich source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
Barks: The barks of the bell pepper plant contain tannins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate frequent urination.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including dried capsicum annuum powder, coconut oil, beeswax, and sweet almond oil.
Combine 20 grams of dried capsicum annuum powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for five minutes at thirty-second intervals stirring between each interval.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and let it cool and set at room temperature for two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild yam creams helps with overactive bladder because they contain diosgenin, a natural compound that has been shown to relax muscles in the urinary tract and reduce muscle spasms. This relaxation effect can help calm an overactive bladder, reducing symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, and incontinence. Additionally, wild yam creams may also help to soothe and calm irritated tissues in the urethra and bladder, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties: Wild yam cream's diuretic properties help reduce urine frequency by increasing urine production, while its anti-inflammatory effects alleviate irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.

Antispasmodic and muscle relaxant properties: The antispasmodic compounds in wild yam cream can help calm down the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing involuntary contractions that contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as urgency and frequency.

Estrogen-like effects: Wild yam contains a compound called diosgenin, which has estrogen-like properties that can help restore balance to the pelvic floor muscles and urethral sphincter, potentially alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder in women.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in diosgenin, a natural steroidal saponin that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in soothing and calming irritated bladders.
Rhizomes: Contains a high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides that help to relax muscle spasms and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dioscorea villosa roots, wash them thoroughly with distilled water, and air-dry for 7 days.
Grind dried dioscorea villosa roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine powdered dioscorea villosa with 20% by weight of petroleum jelly in a heat-proof container and melt in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Remove the heat source and let the mixture cool to room temperature, then whip using an electric mixer for 5 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars, label with date and contents, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months at 3-5°c.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with overactive bladder because they contain natural ingredients that work to soothe and calm the urinary tract muscles, reducing urgency and frequency. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger specifically help to alleviate irritation and discomfort, allowing for more control over the bladder. Additionally, ginger's warming and relaxing effects can also help to ease spasms and contractions that contribute to OAB symptoms. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, ginger creams can provide natural relief from overactive bladder issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like frequency, urgency, and discomfort.

Antispasmodic property: Herbal ginger creams contain compounds that relax smooth muscle tissue, including calcium channel blockers and potassium-channel openers, which can help alleviate spasms and contractions in the bladder and urethra that cause overactive bladder symptoms.

Antioxidant property: Ginger's antioxidant properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to overactive bladder development and progression.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing natural compounds like gingerol and shogaol, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract muscles.
Barks: With their high concentration of bioactive compounds, barks can provide a calming effect on the bladder walls, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and other active compounds, roots have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues, making them a suitable candidate for medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality zingiber officinale roots from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm and dry environment for at least seven days.
Grind dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one part zingiber officinale powder with two parts coconut oil and three parts shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until it reaches 160°f and then cool before filling into jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with overactive bladder because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm the muscles in the urinary tract. By reducing inflammation and spasms, stinging nettle creams can help alleviate symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, and incontinence. Additionally, stinging nettle's ability to relax muscle tone may also help reduce the frequency of bladder contractions, allowing for more comfortable and manageable urination patterns.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination and urgency.
Antioxidant activity: Nettle's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage bladder tissue, helping to prevent oxidative stress and promote overall bladder health and function.

Soothing properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds work together to soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the urinary tract, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, nettle roots help soothe irritated bladder tissue and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: Abundant in bioflavonoids, nettle leaves support urinary tract health by strengthening bladder walls and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and flowers, drying them immediately to preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine 20g of dried herb with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or olive oil.
Steep the herbal mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 70°c to release its bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass jar, filtering out any remaining solids.
Allow the cream to cool, then store it in an airtight container for up to 6 months before using it to alleviate overactive bladder symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with overactive bladder because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to calm the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. The antispasmodic properties of yarrow help to relax the muscles in the bladder, allowing for more efficient storage and reducing the frequency of sudden, strong urges to urinate. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in yarrow cream help to soothe the urethra and surrounding tissues, further alleviating symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Yarrow's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing contractions and frequency of urination.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which may contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as urgency and frequency.
Antioxidant properties: Yarrow's antioxidant properties help protect the mucous membranes of the urinary tract from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing frequency and urgency associated with overactive bladder.
Buds: High in tannins, these small, immature leaves are particularly effective at reducing inflammation and congestion in the urethra and bladder, providing relief from symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium leaves from a trusted source or purchase them online.
Combine 20g of dried achillea millefolium leaves with 100ml of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes steeping time.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass measuring cup and discard solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax pellets with 25g of coconut oil in a double boiler until melted and uniform.
Gradually add 20ml of the strained achillea millefolium infusion to the melted wax and oil mixture while whipping until well combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with overactive bladder because they contain isoflavones, which have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the urinary tract. These compounds work by reducing muscle contractions and improving bladder function, allowing for more efficient storage of urine. Additionally, red clover's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe irritated tissues, further alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder such as urgency and frequency.
remedy_properties: Ispoligonsides: These saponins have a calming effect on the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and soothing irritations that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids in red clover cream have been shown to relax the smooth muscle of the bladder wall, reducing contractions and frequency of urination, which can help alleviate overactive bladder symptoms.

Phytoestrogens: Red clover contains phytoestrogens that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, which can help restore balance to the urinary tract and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder, such as frequency and urgency.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the red clover plant contain triterpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
Buds: The buds of the red clover plant are rich in flavonoids, which can help relax the muscles in the urinary tract and reduce inflammation, providing relief from frequent urination.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh trifolium pratense flowers in the early morning dew and dry them immediately for 7-10 days.
Grind 20 grams of dried trifolium pratense flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered trifolium pratense with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 65°c for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with overactive bladder because of its natural soothing and calming properties. The herbal extract has been traditionally used to relieve urinary tract issues, including urgency and frequency. When applied topically, chamomile cream can help relax the muscles in the pelvic area, reducing spasms and inflammation that can contribute to an overactive bladder. This can lead to a decrease in symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, and nocturia, allowing for improved quality of life and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can reduce inflammation in the bladder lining, alleviating irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder.
Antispasmodic property: Chamomile's flavonoids, like quercetin, have a muscle-relaxing effect that helps to calm spasms in the bladder muscles, reducing frequency of urination and urgency.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's apigenin and other compounds can bind to GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and calming effects on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress related to overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the urinary tract, providing relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds contain flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate irritation in the urinary system, making them useful for treating overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomilla flowers at dawn, ensuring optimal potency and quality.
Steep 10 grams of dried chamomilla flowers in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Combine the infused liquid with 200 milligrams of beeswax, 50 milliliters of coconut oil, and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under gentle simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with overactive bladder because they contain curcumin, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination and urgency. Additionally, curcumin may help relax the muscles in the pelvic floor, reducing the frequency and intensity of bladder contractions that can lead to urinary incontinence. By addressing these underlying issues, turmeric creams can provide natural relief from the discomfort and embarrassment of an overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's potent anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation in the bladder and surrounding tissues, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder such as urgency and frequency.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in turmeric cream, including curcumin, help protect the bladder lining from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like frequent urination.

Muscle relaxant properties: Turmeric's active compounds, such as curcumin and volatile oils, have been shown to relax smooth muscle in the bladder and urinary tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe bladder irritations and reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.

Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in volatile oils and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal creams for overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried turmeric root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the turmeric powder with 10 milliliters of coconut oil and stir well to ensure uniform mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with the turmeric extract and heat again in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for an additional 15 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for overactive bladder are topical products containing natural herbs that help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder, such as frequent urination and incontinence. These creams work by relaxing the bladder muscles and improving bladder control. Examples include creams containing saw palmetto, witch hazel, and aloe vera, which have been shown to reduce symptoms and improve quality of life for individuals with overactive bladder. They can help people regain confidence and independence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overactive bladder that are treated with herbal creams are urinary frequency, stress incontinence, and interstitial cystitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as marshmallow root, uva ursi, and St. John's Wort have been traditionally used to soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to these conditions. They may also help strengthen the muscles around the bladder and urethra, improving bladder control and reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with overactive bladder is a blend of Uva Ursi, Marshmallow, and Aloe Vera. Uva Ursi helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the urinary tract, while Marshmallow provides a protective barrier to prevent further irritation. Aloe Vera, with its anti-inflammatory properties, further calms the bladder and reduces spasms. This combination can help alleviate symptoms, promote relaxation, and support overall bladder health. Regular application may lead to noticeable improvements in bladder control and reduced urgency.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Acta urologica Belgica, 20 patients suffering from idiopathic large hypotonic bladder were treated with extracts of Echinaceae and Sabalae, and a significant decrease was observed in the bladder capacity, residual urine, and compliance, while a significant increase was observed in the detrusor pressure and peak flow. The study used urodynamic investigation (urovideo 2100 and neuromatic 2000M) to evaluate the patients before and after treatment, and the results were analyzed using the STUDENT test for small numbers corrected by the FISHER factor. The findings suggest that herbal creams containing Echinaceae and Sabalae extracts can be beneficial in treating overactive bladder by facilitating neurotransmission and activating smooth muscle fibers, potentially reducing symptoms of incontinence and improving bladder function.
